data.title: CORRECTING and REPLACING SURMOUNT Facilitates $17.7 Million Net Lease Industrial Transaction with PREH Berkshire and Cardinal Industrial
data.description:
CORRECTING and REPLACING SURMOUNT Facilitates $17.7 Million Net Lease Industrial Transaction with PREH Berkshire and Cardinal Industrial

The firm represented both buyer and seller in the acquisition of a 264,793-square-foot industrial facility

Please replace the release dated September 10, 2025 with the following corrected version due to multiple revisions.

The updated release reads:

SURMOUNT FACILITATES $17.7 MILLION NET LEASE INDUSTRIAL TRANSACTION WITH PREH BERKSHIRE AND CARDINAL INDUSTRIAL

The firm represented both buyer and seller in the acquisition of a 264,793-square-foot industrial facility

SURMOUNT (“the firm”), a leading full-service net lease real estate advisory and investment platform, today announced the completion of a $17.7 million net lease industrial transaction in Janesville, Wis. The firm represented both buyer and seller in the acquisition of a 264,793-square-foot industrial facility located at 2505-2533 Foster Avenue by PREH Berkshire LLC from Cardinal Industrial. The deal was facilitated with assistance from Keith Kantrowitz, who served as investment consultant for the buyer, and George Hicker representing Cardinal Industrial as the seller. The transaction, which closed on August 26, 2025, involved two buildings situated on 15.20 acres and represents the third acquisition Surmount has facilitated on behalf of PREH Berkshire.

"This transaction exemplifies our ability to source unique investment opportunities that deliver above-market returns for our clients," said Anthony Bird, a Vice President at SURMOUNT who represented the buyer. "PREH Berkshire came to us seeking confidential investment-grade net lease industrial opportunities with attractive yields, and our team was able to deliver exactly what they were looking for."

The properties are located at the heart of a transportation hub that includes Routes 14, 51 and 26, as well as I-90, providing efficient access to the Milwaukee, Madison and Chicago metropolitan areas – which together comprise roughly 11.9 million residents. Both buildings are occupied by RathGibson, a leading global manufacturer of precision welded tubing and pipe serving the energy, chemical and petrochemical, food and beverage, and pharmaceutical and biopharmaceutical industries, among others.

"This acquisition advances our strategy of acquiring high-quality stabilized assets that deliver consistent, long-term cash flows," said a spokesperson from PREH Berkshire. "We’ve been impressed by SURMOUNT’s ability to source opportunities and facilitate deals that meet our specific investment criteria – this is why we continue to work with the team, and we look forward to evolving the relationship over time."

SURMOUNT Vice President Rocco Driscoll, who represented the seller, added, "This transaction demonstrates the virtuous circle of working with our team as we drove a true win-win for both sides – the seller generated a compelling risk-adjusted return on investment, while the buyer was able to acquire supremely well located, stabilized assets on an attractive basis.”

About SURMOUNT

SURMOUNT is a full-service commercial real estate firm offering global expertise in net lease advisory, sale leaseback, lease structuring, development, capital markets, and principal investments. With over 130 professionals across offices in New York City, Boston, Scottsdale, and Chicago, SURMOUNT provides end-to-end real estate services for private investors, REITs, private equity and financial sponsors, family offices, multi-unit franchisees, business owners, tenants, developers, and lenders.

Media Contact:
Media@Surmount.com

View source version on businesswire.com: https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20250910927141/en/

data.title: Dow Jones Top Company Headlines at 3 PM ET: Winklevoss-Backed Gemini Space Soars in Latest IPO Market Win | Rivian ...
data.description:
Winklevoss-Backed Gemini Space Soars in Latest IPO Market Win

The crypto exchange jumped 43% in volatile trading in its Nasdaq debut, the latest upside surprise in the resurgent IPO market.

----

Rivian Has a Recall, Tesla Style. The Stock Is Down.

The EV maker is recalling 24,214 vehicles to correct an issue with its driver-assistance features. An over-the-air update to its software is expected to fix the problem.

----

Uber Faces Justice Department Suit for Alleged Disabilities Act Violations

The Justice Department's complaint alleges that Uber and its drivers routinely refused to serve individuals with disabilities and imposed surcharges such as cleaning fees for service animal shedding and cancellation fees to riders it has denied service.

----

Boeing Gets Proposed Fine From FAA for Alleged Violations Tied to Door Plug Blowout

The FAA hit Boeing with proposed fines of more than $3.1 million for alleged safety violations that occurred from September 2023 to February 2024.

----

Homebuilders in Tight Spot as Profits Sink, Regional Markets Shift

Companies are facing reduced margins thanks to surplus inventory in some regions and high demand in others.

----

Apple Postpones iPhone Air's Release in China

The company is awaiting regulatory approval for its new, thinner iPhone model in its second-largest market.

----

CEO of Tylenol Maker Lobbied RFK Jr. Not to Cite Drug as Autism Cause in Report

In a hastily scheduled meeting, Kenvue interim Chief Executive Kirk Perry sought to dissuade the health secretary, arguing there is no clear link.

----

Armani's Will Seeks Sale of Fashion Empire or Public Listing

Instructions from the late Italian designer to his heirs aim to shake up the independent label's ownership.

----

Norwegian Wealth Fund Excludes Miner Eramet From Portfolio Over Ethics Concerns

The ethics council advising the fund said there was a risk that Eramet could be contributing to environmental damage and human-rights violations.

----

Microsoft Settles EU Antitrust Probe Over Teams After Officials Accept Concessions

The company is to make suites available without Teams at a reduced price, let some customers switch to suites without Teams, and will provide function interoperability.

----

Joby and Archer Aviation Jump Because President Trump Likes Flying Cars

The companies will participate in a White House program that can help speed the adoption of electric vertical takeoff and landing aircraft.

----

RH Stock Falls as Tariff Uncertainty Hits Earnings Outlook. What to Know.

The home furnishing retailer said tariffs would add to its costs next year.

----

CVC Strikes $1.5 Billion Deal for GoDaddy Rival Namecheap

The buyout firm is poised to acquire a majority stake in the Arizona-based domain registrar and web-hosting provider.

----

A U.S. Icon Loses Out to European Rival in Rush for Air Defenses

----

If Pepsi Wants to Win, It Has to Play Coke's Game

Coke shed bottling and poured money into marketing. Unless Pepsi slims down, the gap between the two will continue to widen.

----

Freight Startup Flexport Forecasts Profitability in 2025-With an Asterisk

data.title: Dow Jones Top Markets Headlines at 3 PM ET: Dow Slips After Weaker Sentiment Data | Consumer ...
data.description:
Dow Slips After Weaker Sentiment Data

Stocks were mixed, with the Dow industrials and Nasdaq composite moving in opposite directions. All three major U.S. indexes are on track for gains of over 1% for the week, with the Nasdaq on pace for another record close.

----

Consumer sentiment hits four-month low in September as lower- and middle-income Americans worry about jobs

Consumers were worried about rising risks to business conditions and labor markets, as well as increasing inflation.

----

USDA Raises Corn Production Outlook to Record

The USDA forecasts 16.81 billion bushels of corn to be produced by farmers in the 2025-26 marketing year, well above the 2023 record high of 15.34 billion bushels.

----

Moderna, Pfizer stocks fall as Trump officials reportedly link COVID shots to child deaths

Shares of companies that make COVID-19 vaccines sank Friday, after a report raised fears that Trump administration health officials will blame the vaccines for some child deaths.

----

Trump Pushes to Remove Fed's Lisa Cook Before Next Policy Meeting

The Federal Open Market Committee meets next week to decide on a widely expected quarter-point rate cut.

----

U.K. Targets Russia With New Sanctions on Oil Fleet, Defense Suppliers

One hundred sanctions against Russia target ships carrying Russian oil as well as companies supplying electronics, chemicals and explosives used to manufacture weapons.

----

EU Won't Shy Away From Tough Antitrust Enforcement, Competition Chief Says

The European Union is ready to hand hefty fines to companies that breach antitrust rules despite risks of raising tensions with the U.S. at a delicate time for trade, the bloc's top competition enforcer said.

----

Beijing Warns Mexico Against Tariffs That Could Harm Chinese Goods

Beijing has warned Mexico to think twice before moving ahead with a plan to raise tariffs that could hurt Chinese automakers, saying it would retaliate against the measure.

----

U.K. Economy Stagnated in July

Gross domestic product ground to a halt in July, matching consensus estimates, though the Bank of England was still expected to persist with rate cuts.

----

Russia's Central Bank Cuts Key Rate for Third Consecutive Meeting

The Bank of Russia cut its key rate to 17% from 18% as the economy and inflation slowed following two years of rapid expansion driven by government spending on the war in Ukraine.

----

Front and Center in This Week's IPOs: Individual Investors

Klarna, Gemini and others have set aside more shares for everyday buyers.

----

Stocks and bonds are sending very different messages about recession risks

The stock market keeps touching fresh record highs, while the bond market is girding for trouble in the economy. 




  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  September 12, 2025 15:15 ET (19:15 GMT)


Copyright (c) 2025 Dow Jones & Company, Inc.

data.title: Knoxville to Sell $45.3M of Revenue Bonds to Fund Purchase of Apartment Complex
data.description:
By Chris Wack 




  The Industrial Development Board of the City of Knoxville plans to sell $45.3 million of revenue bonds to finance the purchase of an apartment complex. 



  The board will loan the proceeds of the Series 2025 bonds to TUFF UT Park LLC, a Georgia limited liability company, according to documents posted on MuniOS. 



  TUFF UT Park will use the proceeds to buy a 199-unit apartment complex known as Maplehurst Park Apartments in Knoxville, Tenn. 



  The bonds will bear interest at a fixed rate and mature on Nov. 1, 2030. Interest will be payable on each May 1 and Nov. 1, beginning May 1, 2026. 



  Interest rates and yields for the bonds have not been set. 



  The apartments are primarily marketed and rented to graduate students of the University of Tennessee. Typical leases are 12 months, and current occupancy is about 97%. 



  TUFF UT Park has and will have no other assets other than the apartment project. It will have no sources of revenue other than payments received from tenants of the project. 



  Truist is listed as the lead manager of the offering. 





  Write to Chris Wack at chris.wack@wsj.com 





  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  September 12, 2025 15:05 ET (19:05 GMT)


Copyright (c) 2025 Dow Jones & Company, Inc.

data.title: Psyx Research, Inc. : Psyx Expands Audio Portfolio With Optimized 3D Rendering For Cadence Tensilica HiFi DSPs
data.description:
Psyx Research, Inc. : Psyx Expands Audio Portfolio With Optimized 3D Rendering For Cadence Tensilica HiFi DSPs

Psyx Research, Inc., a leader in immersive audio solutions and audio enhancement technologies, today announced the availability of Psyx’s next generation hemispherical rendering algorithms on Cadence Tensilica HiFi DSPs for Audio, Voice, Speech and AI, enabling rapid integration of Psyx algorithms to support immersive playback of all stereo content, without the need for physical height channels.

Psyx’s first-to-market wireless immersive audio solutions on Bluetooth SoCs utilizing the family of Tensilica HiFi DSPs are shipping now worldwide. Licensees are able to develop products ranging from headphones to multichannel systems with four or more virtual height channels, compatible with all legacy stereo content, including vinyl. Psyx’s new virtual height image map technology extracts and renders height information in real time to devices with any number of channels or configuration, removing the need for physical height speakers or specific mixing formats that depend on metadata for sound positioning.

“We have seen recent rapid expansion of the immersive audio market despite the technology and infrastructure barriers to delivery. Our hemispherical rendering system was developed with interoperability at its core, ensuring discrete three-dimensional playback from any stereo source - no metadata or encoder required,” said Mark Reasinger, CEO of Psyx Research, Inc. “Cadence Tensilica HiFi DSPs are integral to our global ecosystem across multiple markets and semiconductor partners, making them an easy choice for our first implementations of Psyx 3D. Our strong relationship with Cadence continues to result in faster time-to-market and optimum performance for our customers.”

Psyx engineers find Cadence’s Tensilica HiFi DSPs and Tensilica Xtensa Processors instrumental in bringing Psyx’s advanced algorithms to market. Cycle-accurate simulation enables rapid profiling and optimization of core functions, while the tools provide full functional visibility into the processing budget at all hierarchical levels. Leveraging Tensilica HiFi DSP intrinsics within Psyx’s proprietary libraries enable more operations per cycle, delivering maximum performance, while the highly optimized NatureDSP library supports additional advanced mathematical operations. The Tensilica HiFi DSP architecture is well-structured, thoroughly documented, and straightforward to apply, enabling seamless integration of Psyx immersive technologies.

“With its industry-leading performance and energy profile, the Cadence Tensilica HiFi DSP family is an ideal platform for pioneering innovation in audio, voice, speech and AI applications at the edge,” said Prakash Madhvapathy, Product Marketing Director at Cadence. “Our partnership with Psyx has enabled them to offer efficient implementations of their metadata-free 3D rendering algorithms to run on the vast deployment of Tensilica HiFi DSPs in headphones, TWS earbuds, TVs, soundbars, and smart speakers. Consumers no longer content with the stereo listening experience can immerse themselves in rich three-dimensional sound rendered through a variety of speaker configurations.”

Psyx Hemispherical Rendering Benefits:

  • Next generation image map technology delivers discrete 3D image regardless of the number of input or output channels
  • No metadata required
  • No encoding required
  • All Tensilica HiFi DSPs supported

Licensees leveraging Tensilica HiFi DSPs for audio also benefit from Psyx’s tuning and integration support, ensuring the highest possible quality of experience on devices powered by Psyx technology.

For more information about Psyx, please visit www.psyxresearch.com or contact support at info@psyxaudio.com.

About Psyx Research, Inc.

Psyx Research, Inc. is a leading developer of patented audio solutions for every stage in the ecosystem from creation to consumption. The company’s groundbreaking immersive audio solutions bridge the gap between next-generation technologies and legacy content. Employing proprietary psychoacoustic modeling and image map-based rendering, Psyx draws on over 50 years of expertise in developing real-time simulation and audio rendering to deliver the most realistic listening experiences possible regardless of content, era, format, or delivery method. Headquartered in Washington, the company maintains offices in the U.S. and Asia.

info@psyxaudio.com

data.title: MCAP Inc. Announces $0.10 Dividend Per Share
data.description:
MCAP Inc. Announces $0.10 Dividend Per Share

MCAP Inc. Announces $0.10 Dividend Per Share

PR Newswire

ORLANDO, Fla., Sept. 12, 2025

ORLANDO, Fla., Sept. 12, 2025 /PRNewswire/ -- MCAP Inc. (OTC: MCAP) today announced a $0.10 dividend.

MCAP's Board of Directors approved and declared a dividend of $0.10 per common share. The dividend is payable on October 1, 2025, to shareholders of record as of the close of business September 17, 2025.

About MCAP Inc.

MCAP Inc. is a U.S. based public holding company owning subsidiaries and investments in various financial technology companies. MCAP Inc. owns, develops, and invests in the electronic market making and execution sector, securities trading technologies, and algo execution platforms.

MCAP continues to expand through its investment in customer relationships, application software development, securities execution platforms and diversification of business lines. The Company also seeks M&A opportunities that are aligned with the Company's core competencies in order to increase long-term shareholder value.

Please visit our website: www.mcapmarkets.com.

Forward-Looking Statements

This release contains forward-looking statements within the meaning of the safe harbor provisions of the Private Securities Litigation Reform Act of 1995, as amended. The matters discussed in this news release involve goals, forecasts, assumptions, risks, and uncertainties that could cause actual results to differ materially from those expressed in the forward-looking statements. Earnings are adjusted non-GAAP. Readers are cautioned not to place undue reliance on forward-looking statements.

data.title: Arkansas Lawsuit Filed Over Canceled "Burial" Life Insurance Policies Sold by Globe Life
data.description:
Arkansas Lawsuit Filed Over Canceled "Burial" Life Insurance Policies Sold by Globe Life

Arkansas Lawsuit Filed Over Canceled "Burial" Life Insurance Policies Sold by Globe Life

PR Newswire

LITTLE ROCK, Ark., Sept. 12, 2025

LITTLE ROCK, Ark., Sept. 12, 2025 /PRNewswire/ -- Jennings & Earley has filed a class action lawsuit in the U.S. District Court for the Eastern District of Arkansas. The lawsuit says Globe Life and Accident Insurance Company canceled some small life insurance policies that families buy to help pay funeral costs (often called "burial policies") and then denied related claims. The complaint says these actions violate Arkansas law, including Ark. Code § 23‑79‑107. These are allegations only and have not been proven in court.

What the lawsuit says

  • Globe Life "rescinded" certain policies — that means treating a policy as if it never existed — based on statements from the application.
  • The complaint claims those cancellations and claim denials did not follow Arkansas rules for when and how a policy may be canceled after it is issued.
  • The lawsuit asks the Court for relief allowed by law. That may include stopping the challenged practices and paying money to affected Arkansans.

Who could be affected

  • Arkansans who bought Globe Life burial policies, or their beneficiaries, and who had a policy canceled or a related claim denied.

Case information

  • Caption: Debra Jennings v. Globe Life and Accident Insurance Company
  • Court: U.S. District Court, Eastern District of Arkansas
  • Case No.: 2:25-cv-00172-BSM
  • Filed: September 3, 2025

Learn more

  • Firm updates and contact form: https://www.jefirm.com
  • Arkansas insurance statute: Ark. Code § 23‑79‑107

This release summarizes allegations in a publicly filed complaint. The Court will decide the outcome.

View original content to download multimedia:https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/arkansas-lawsuit-filed-over-canceled-burial-life-insurance-policies-sold-by-globe-life-302555399.html

SOURCE Jennings & Earley PLLC

data.title: LTK Announces 2025 LTK Award Winners at Annual Creator Conference
data.description:
LTK Announces 2025 LTK Award Winners at Annual Creator Conference

LTK, the company that turned influence into a global economy, announced the annual LTK Award winners at LTK Con 2025, the company’s 13th annual creator conference. The awards spotlight the creators shaping culture and commerce by building loyal communities on LTK and the brands redefining creator partnerships through strategic collaborations.

This press release features multimedia. View the full release here: https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20250912906902/en/

LTK announced the annual LTK Award winners at LTK Con 2025, the company’s 13th annual creator conference. The awards spotlight the creators shaping culture and commerce by building loyal communities on LTK and the brands redefining creator partnerships through strategic collaborations.

This year’s creator winners exemplify the power of authentic storytelling, creativity, and trust to inspire millions. The LTK Awards also recognize brands that maximize creator-led marketing, raising awareness, driving measurable impact, and building long-term connections with both creators and consumers.

At its core, LTK is designed to strengthen relationships between creators, brands, and their audiences, making it the trusted destination powered by real people, real content, and meaningful connections.

LTK Video Creator of the Year: Danielle Bernstein, @weworewhat

Style LTK Creator

  • All Star Winner: Kristal Heredia, @kristalheredia
  • Rising Star Winner: Alexis Wilkerson, @awilkyy

Beauty LTK Creator

  • All Star Winner: Jourdan Sloane, @jourdansloane
  • Rising Star Winner: Ashley Wagner, @Ashley.Paiige

Home LTK Creator

  • All Star Winner: Kristina Evans, @kristinanevans
  • Rising Star Winner: Ana Egger, @Staged_By_Ana_Egger

Family LTK Creator

  • All Star Winner: Jennifer Reimold, @jennyreimold
  • Rising Star Winner: Katryce Pedro, @Katryce_Pedro

Active LTK Creator

  • All Star Winner: Nicky Schmaderer, @nickyschmaderer_fit
  • Rising Star Winner: Samantha Christine Huskey, @samcfit

Travel LTK Creator

  • All Star Winner: Dana Berez, @danaberez
  • Rising Star Winner: Yhasmina Tiphaine, @Yhasmina

Food LTK Creator

  • All Star Winner: Tieghan Gerard, @halfbakedharvest
  • Rising Star Winner: Jessica Secrest, @JessAggressiveTutorials

Visionary of the Year: The direct-to-consumer businesses that have successfully revolutionized industries on their own terms, leveraging LTK Creators to drive sales, traffic and brand recognition in inspiring ways.

  • Winner: Z Supply
  • Nominees: Comfrt, Current Body

Best Multi-Category Campaign Strategy: The brand with the best multi-category campaign strategy, breaking through various verticals through strategic marketing and creator approaches.

  • Winner: Nordstrom
  • Nominees: Amazon, The Home Depot

One to Watch: Recognizes the brand expanding their reach on LTK through a variety of engaging approaches and continues to invest in ground-breaking strategies, reaching new shoppers and driving incremental sales.

  • Winner: Vuori
  • Nominees: Etsy, GAP

Brand Builder of the Year: The "Brand Builder of the Year" award recognizes the brand that has demonstrated exceptional skill and creativity in developing, enhancing, and expanding their brand. This award celebrates those who have not only established a strong brand presence but also significantly contributed to its growth and reputation over the past year.

  • Winner: Ulta
  • Nominees: Levi's, Spanx, Ulta

Best 360 Campaign Strategy: Through a 360 do-it-all strategic approach, these brands have maximized budgets, scale and reach to Creators and shoppers through successful collaborations, LTK advertising, and Media Boosting.

  • Winner: Stanley
  • Nominees: adidas, Evereve

Best Category Exclusive - Fashion: This award recognizes an exceptional brand campaign that stands out within its specific category through unique and exclusive attributes.

  • Winner: Coach
  • Nominee: Abercrombie + Fitch, Tuckernuck

Best Performance Strategy: Through a strategic approach with LTK Creators, these brands achieved some of the highest performing campaigns—combining strategic testing of channels, variety of Creators and content formats.

  • Winner: J.Crew
  • Nominees: DSW, Free People

Best Retail Media Network: This award honors a retail media platform that has demonstrated exceptional performance and innovation in connecting brands with consumers. This award celebrates excellence in leveraging data, technology, and creativity to deliver impactful advertising solutions that drive measurable results for both retailers and brands.

  • Winner: Best Buy
  • Nominees: Nordstrom Media Network, Saks RMN

About LTK

LTK is the global creator commerce platform that turned creator influence into a worldwide economy. Founded in 2011 by Amber Venz Box and Baxter Box, LTK is a three-sided marketplace with dedicated platforms for creators, brands, and consumers.

LTK empowers creators to retain, nurture and grow direct relationships with their audiences through their LTK profiles, creating lasting businesses. For brands, LTK is a full-funnel, performance channel that scales globally, and turns creators into a retailer’s tech-enabled power partner. Today, LTK powers $6B in annual retail sales, supports 60,000 creator-brand collaborations each year, has 7,000 retailers actively investing in creators, and is used by more than 40M monthly users. LTK’s mission to empower creator success has led to nearly $3B invested in creators and helped nearly 400 women become self-made millionaires.

Headquartered in Dallas, TX, LTK operates across five continents. Download the LTK app in the App Store or Google Play. For updates, follow @ltk.hq on Instagram and @shopltk on X and LTK on LinkedIn.

press@rewardstyle.com
